title: Post-CABG follow-up

red_flags.text: Supports a clear post-MI management plan, blood-pressure and renal monitoring, and bleeding-risk assessment at follow-up.
red_flags.rationale: Secondary-prevention follow-up was reviewed only for people whose CABG followed myocardial infarction; it was not used to infer that context.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports a clear post-MI management plan, blood-pressure and renal monitoring, and bleeding-risk assessment at follow-up.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.4.1–1.4.3 and 1.4.10 — secondary prevention and follow-up monitoring

investigations.text: Supports review of investigation results and future management from discharge documentation, exercise and functional context, cardiac rehabilitation, and structured follow-up.
investigations.rationale: NG185 supports documenting discharge information, recovery and rehabilitation context, symptom change, patient questions, and arranged follow-up after an acute coronary syndrome; CABG-specific operative and graft details remain outside scope.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports review of investigation results and future management from discharge documentation, exercise and functional context, cardiac rehabilitation, and structured follow-up.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.7.1–1.8.34 — discharge information and cardiac rehabilitation

additions.text: Supports review of investigation results and future management from discharge documentation, exercise and functional context, cardiac rehabilitation, and structured follow-up.
additions.rationale: NG185 supports documenting discharge information, recovery and rehabilitation context, symptom change, patient questions, and arranged follow-up after an acute coronary syndrome; CABG-specific operative and graft details remain outside scope.
additions.evidence_extract: Supports review of investigation results and future management from discharge documentation, exercise and functional context, cardiac rehabilitation, and structured follow-up.

source_limitations: Operation date and indication, grafts, sternotomy and wound, complications, discharge medicines, adherence, exercise tolerance, angina, breathlessness, edema, rhythm, rehabilitation, investigations, clinician assessment, treatment, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
